summary: Detect cyrus-sasl module availability
Misformatted test-results.json on POWER8
bson_init_from_data() missing
Document configuration options in installation docs
description:
We should list, and document, each configuration option of libbson and mongoc. Both standard configure options and our custom ones.

--

libbson's README.md has info on config options like `--enable-static`.  libmongoc's README does not, nor does the mongoc docs site.

I think it would help users to mention that and other common autolib/configure options in an easy-to-find location.

summary: Document thread safety for mongoc_client_pool functions
description:
The docs for mongoc_client_pool_set_apm_callbacks say:

{quote}
This function can only be called once on a pool, and must be called before the first mongoc_client_pool_pop.
{quote}

There's no check for whether or not a client has been popped already in this function, so I think we should add that.

This might also be kind of a silly point, but I think this function should lock the client pool mutex, or (maybe) be described as "not thread safe" in the docs. In reality I'm sure nobody calls this function from multiple threads, but if someone did, the behavior would be weird.

{{mongoc_client_pool_set_error_api}} should probably lock the pool's mutex or be described as "not thread safe" in the docs as well. I'm guessing nobody actually calls this from multiple threads either, so I don't think it's a "real" bug, but it does look a bit strange.

summary: Improve mongoc_init and mongoc_cleanup docs
description:
*Update*: to close this ticket, ensure that it's documented that mongoc_cleanup should be called once, and no other C Driver functions, not even mongoc_init, can be called after. Also, add links between mongoc_init, mongoc_cleanup, and the index page. Right now [mongoc_cleanup|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/mongoc_cleanup.html] is isolated.

After a long debugging tour I learned
that calling mongoc_init() and mongoc_cleanup() multiple times is bad practice
which might result in segmentation faults.

I suggest hereby adding

assert(once==MONGOC_ONCE_INIT)

to the implemetnations of these two functions,
for indicating that there might be a problem. 

Regards
Andre

summary: readConcern option should not be included in getMore commands
description:
In [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#516|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/516], a user encountered the following error when iterating on a large result set on a {{find}} operation that included a "local" read concern level:

bq. Failed to parse: { getMore: 44504619209605, collection: "customers", readConcern: { level: "local" } }. Unrecognized field 'readConcern'.

If we look at [{{_mongoc_cursor_cursorid_get_more()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.3/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor-cursorid.c#L224], it calls [{{_mongoc_cursor_prepare_getmore_command()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.3/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor-cursorid.c#L175] to create the {{getMore}} command document. That document is then passed along to [{{_mongoc_cursor_cursorid_refresh_from_command()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.3/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor-cursorid.c#L108] and executed with [{{_mongoc_cursor_run_command()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.3/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor.c#L108], wherein a {{readConcern}} field [may be appended|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.3/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor.c#L1248]. The logic for appending the {{readConcern}} needs to be a bit more restrictive so that it only triggers for supported commands.

summary: Bump the handshake configure flags
description:
I am pretty sure I forgot to bump the handshake bitmask after adding new configure flags in mongoc-config.h.in
This needs to be fixed before the release.

summary: Deprecate mongoc_collection_save
description: Move closer to CRUD Spec compliance and uniformity with other drivers.

summary: mongoc_collection_update() omits validation entirely for update documents
Document maxAwaitTimeMS option for "find"
Crash creating index when unable to connect
Autotools may skip substitution of MONGOC_HAVE_SOCKLEN
description: Per [this GitHub comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/6c63c20006602d5561689ebff39d9a61cfe60451#commitcomment-20697467], it looks like the {{AC_CHECK_TYPE}} for {{socket_t}} may leave {{MONGOC_HAVE_SOCKLEN}} unreplaced if the check fails. Contrast that with the CMake scripts, which do replace it with {{0}} if the type is not found.

summary: Move MONGOC_ENABLE_SASL_SSPI guard up in mongoc-cluster-sspi.c
description:
Per [this GitHub comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/96dd6ed98d50f66ce4667d117b341b2d7103e97d#commitcomment-20697810], the {{#ifdef MONGOC_ENABLE_SASL_SSPI}} guard should be moved above the private header includes, as is done for other C files with conditional code (e.g. {{mongoc-cluster-sasl.c}}). As-is, it's possible the trigger the following build error when including this C file when SSPI was not found (i.e. {{MONGOC_ENABLE_SASL_SSPI}} is undefined):

bq. src/mongoc/mongoc-sspi-private.h:32:21: fatal error: Windows.h: No such file or directory

summary: Implement Connection String Spec Tests
Document all supported connection URI options
description:
There are few undocumented URI options:

- appname
- authMechanism
- authMechanismProperties (canonicalizeHostname, gssapiServiceName)
- maxStalenessSeconds

summary: skip test_create_index_fail when offline
description: tests/test-mongoc-collection.c:4755 test_create_index_fail(): : [No suitable servers found (`serverSelectionTryOnce` set): [Failed to resolve 'example.com']] does not contain with [connection timeout]

summary: Create bson_ascii_str[n]casecmp()
description:
We use {{strcasecmp}} a fair amount in mongoc and therefore have a quick windows-workaround for it in [src/mongoc/mongoc-util-private.h|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/master/src/mongoc/mongoc-util-private.h#L26].

libbson has couple of uses for {{strcasecmp}} now to, so it makes sense to unify these all in libbson.

To that end, we should create {{bson_ascii_strcasecmp}}()

summary: ".doctree" files included in distribution tarball
description: Since we switched from Mallard to Sphinx for generating our HTML and man page documentation, "make dist" now calls sphinx-build and generates cache files in "doc/html/.doctrees". I've manually removed these from the distribution for 1.6.0. Let's figure out how to update our Makefile.am scripts to exclude these files from the tarball automatically.

summary: Compiler warnings on mingw-w64 (gcc)
description:
Building on windows 7 with mingw-w64 a few issues come up:

Found the following significant warnings:
{code}
  yajl/yajl_gen.c:205:5: warning: too many arguments for format [-Wformat-extra-args]
  bson/bson-error.c:111:4: warning: implicit declaration of function 'strerror_s' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
  mongoc/mongoc-socket.c:1131:7: warning: implicit declaration of function 'inet_ntop' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
  mongoc/mongoc-ssl.c:224:4: warning: implicit declaration of function 'inet_pton' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
{code}
Moreover, a linking error that only appears when building for 32bit, it does not appear for x64 (I am already including -lws2_32):
{code}
./libmongoc.a(mongoc-socket.o):mongoc-socket.c:(.text+0x1373): undefined reference to `inet_ntop'
./libmongoc.a(mongoc-socket.o):mongoc-socket.c:(.text+0x13b3): undefined reference to `inet_ntop'
./libmongoc.a(mongoc-ssl.o):mongoc-ssl.c:(.text+0x3bc): undefined reference to `inet_pton'
collect2: ld returned 1 exit status
{code}
I am currently working around this by shipping my own implementation of inet_ntop and inet_pton from but it would be great if this would be fixed in the library. Some example implementations: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/15660203/inet-pton-identifier-not-found

summary: Inconsistent error reporting for insert, update, and replace BSON validation
Install CMake 'config' style files for find_package
description:
In order to use libbson and libmongo-c easily within CMake projects, you need to find them somehow.

The easiest way to do this is to use the CMake pkg_check_modules (https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.1/module/FindPkgConfig.html) functionality, and that works just fine on platforms that support pkg-config.

However, not all platforms commonly use pkg-config, notably windows.

On Windows, it is much more likely that packages will be found via a project local 'module' for libbson and libmongoc. That module will in turn use find_package (https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.0/command/find_package.html) to look for an installed .cmake file for each of libbson and libmongoc.

Typically, those files would be generated as part of the libbson and libmongoc build process. See https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.0/module/CMakePackageConfigHelpers.html for details on what is expected in those files. As noted on that page, a project built with CMake can use some helpers to generate its config-mode package definition .cmake files

It is unclear whether those files need to be generated from an autoconf build of libbson and libmongoc. Any system where autoconf runs almost certainly has pkg-config. So, maybe libmongoc and libbson can simplify things and only generate and install the config-mode package definition files from the CMake build.

summary: cmake doesn't check libbson minimum version
description: Compiling mongoc using the CMake manifest will happily compile against whatever libbson is installed without verifying its minimum required version.

summary: test-libbson starts too many threads
description:
The libbson test suite fails on 32-bit MIPS and 32-bit MIPS Little Endian:

https://buildd.debian.org/status/package.php?p=libbson&suite=sid

The bug was reported to me originally against 1.5.4. In fact the tests had failed since 1.5.0 but the  since the Debian maintainers didn't catch up on their backlog and file a bug related to the failure immediately. I should ensure I get emails about future test failures.

Roberto Sanchez and I logged into a MIPS machine (I believe it was a 64-bit MIPS big endian?) in the Debian autobuilder network and were able to reproduce the crash: late in the test suite, test-libbson aborts with return code 134. We could fix it by commenting-out a dozen tests; but it didn't seem to matter which ones we commented out. We could also fix the tests by running them single-threaded.

The threaded test runner starts a thread per test and runs them all at once. I hypothesize that we added enough tests between 1.4.2 and 1.5.0 that we hit a limit on the number of threads a MIPS process can start. There are almost 250 tests total.

To fix:

* Update the parallel runner to limit the number of concurrent threads
* Don't run in threaded mode by default, anyway - it could make it hard to diagnose future build failures

Debian Stretch has just entered a freeze and will be released soon. We have libbson and libmongoc 1.4.2 in Stretch, and we won't be able to get anything new included, since this build failure was introduced circa 1.5.0. I think that's ok, the new features in libbson and libmongoc were MongoDB 3.4 support and native TLS on non-Linux, not having these in Debian is fine. Regardless, there's no urgency about fixing this now, we missed the deadline for 1.5.x in Stretch.

The next Debian release, Buster, will be frozen 18 to 24 months from now. Its timeline will be announced soon.

summary: Correct connectTImeoutMS
description:
The C Driver's topology scanner has a hard limit of connectTimeoutMS that spans the period between calling mongoc_async_run to begin discovering, and when mongoc_async_run finishes. Some requests might begin in the middle of the call to mongoc_async_run, because we start checking a server newly discovered in the host list of an isMaster response immediately, but we don't reset the timer when we do so.

This ignores connectTimeoutMS's purpose: it's not for limiting the duration of mongoc_async_run, it's for distinguishing between a server that's slow to respond vs. a server that's down.

Track the duration of server checks per-server, instead of for the entire event loop. Let the loop run a bit longer to give newly discovered servers a fair timeout.

Note, mongoc_async_run's original design tried to do this. Then it had an awkward phase when it enforced timeouts both per-server and for the entire function call. Then I rewrote it to only enforce the timeout for the whole function. That was a mistake, instead we should get rid of the whole-function timeout and only track timeouts per-server.

summary: libbson should ensure regex options are sorted
description: libbson doesn't appear to validate the options string.  It just passes through the  exact cstring without checking that only valid letters are included (i.e. "ismxlu").

summary: NaN and Infinity differently JSON-encoded depending on platform
Raise BSON_MAX_RECURSION from 100 to 200
description: Test parsing and generating a 200-level JSON document. Consider adding such a test to the specs repo.

summary: assert in test_hint_no_warmup_pooled
description:
https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_c_driver_power8_rhel71_test_3.4_replica_set_noauth_sasl_nossl_2eb4b266f30a9fecbf7b72b98e93377604235ed9_17_02_26_22_15_30
{code}
src/mongoc/mongoc-topology-scanner.c:671 mongoc_topology_scanner_start(): precondition failed: !node->cmd
{code}
In the test configuration that caused the assert, we have a 3-node replica set: primary on port 27017, secondary on 27018, arbiter on 27019.

The test creates a client pool with this connection string:

"mongodb://localhost:27017,localhost:27018/?replicaset=replset&serverselectiontryonce=false"

Note that the arbiter on port 27019 is missing from the seed list.

The test pops a client from the pool. Popping the client triggers the pool to begin the background scanner thread (CDRIVER-1333).

The test creates a cursor and sets the cursor's id to 1. (In this setup, server 1 is usually primary.) The test calls mongoc_cursor_next, which calls down to mongoc_cluster_fetch_stream_pooled, which creates a stream connected to the primary and calls _mongoc_cluster_add_node, which calls _mongoc_cluster_run_ismaster.

Once the isMaster completes, the driver calls _mongoc_topology_update_from_handshake to update its topology description (CDRIVER-562).

If (rarely, apparently) _mongoc_topology_update_from_handshake beats the background scanner thread to the first ismaster, then it's the first function to discover the arbiter and add it to the topology scanner. It does so, and calls mongoc_topology_scanner_add_and_scan, which creates a scanner node for the arbiter and initializes node->cmd, the scanner node's async_cmd_t representing the isMaster command it wants to execute.

Then (again, this is a rare race outcome) the background thread begins and sets up scanner nodes for all known servers. By now the arbiter is in the topology description so the thread attempts to call isMaster on it. But first, it asserts that the arbiter's scanner node doesn't already have an async_cmd_t. In this sequence, the scanner's assert fails.

Solution: when discovering a node from _mongoc_topology_update_from_handshake, don't set its async_cmd_t. That's the job of the topology scanner.

summary: Compiler warnings in test-bson.c
localThresholdMS should default to 15 milliseconds
description: The server selection spec states that localThresholdMS should [default to 15 milliseconds|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection.rst#localthresholdms], but [{{MONGOC_TOPOLOGY_LOCAL_THRESHOLD_MS}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-topology-private.h#L30] is defined as 15000 (i.e. 15 seconds). In practice, this causes [{{mongoc_topology_description_suitable_servers()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-topology-description.c#L702] to append most every server to the list before random selection.

summary: mongoc_uri_get_option_as_int32() does not allow a localThresholdMS of zero
description:
The [server selection spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection.rst#nearest] states the following:

bq. To always select the server with the lowest RTT, users should use mode 'nearest' without {{tag_sets}} or {{maxStalenessSeconds}} and set {{localThresholdMS}} to zero.

[{{mongoc_topology_new()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-topology.c#L182] initializes its copy of  {{localThresholdMS}} by [calling {{mongoc_uri_get_option_as_int32()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-topology.c#L256]. which does not respect a zero value and instead returns the fallback of 15ms (as [documented|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-uri.c#L1483]).

One solution may be to create a separate getter that respects zero, as was done with [{{_mongoc_uri_get_max_staleness_option()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.6.0/src/mongoc/mongoc-uri.c#L1014].

summary: localThresholdMS is not validated as a non-negative value
description: This is related to CDRIVER-2059, where a parsed "localThresholdMS" option is treated as {{int32_t}}, {{int64_t}}, and {{size_t}} in various places. This is a separate ticket to propose the addition of validation logic for the URI option, independent of how it is passed around within the topology component.

summary: bson_api.3 and bson_index.3 man pages include all other pages
description: A bug in the port of our man pages from Mallard to Sphinx: the existing man page bson_index.3 and the new page bson_api.3 now include all other documentation. Same for mongoc. Consider not generating these man pages at all, I don't think they're useful.

summary: Using a filter or projection with empty field names causes a crash when destroying cursor
Coverage and scan-build build in mongo-c-driver-release failing
description: The coverage and scan-build datafiles uploading is failing with system error due to permission errors.

summary: Windows static build is broken in 1.5.0
description:
As of libmongoc 1.5.0, attempting to link applications against the static build of the driver will fail with the error that symbols like "\_\_imp_bson_iter_init" and "\_\_imp_mongoc_read_prefs_new" cannot be resolved.

Original description:
{quote}
Linking with bson-static-1.0.lib, mongoc-static-1.0.lib, libbsoncxx.lib, libmongocxx.lib worked in version 3.0.1 but fails with 3.1.0:

{noformat}
1>libbsoncxx.lib(view.cpp.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_init
1>libbsoncxx.lib(core.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_init
1>libbsoncxx.lib(view.cpp.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_init
1>libbsoncxx.lib(view.cpp.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_init_find
1>libbsoncxx.lib(view.cpp.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_init_find
1>libbsoncxx.lib(view.cpp.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __imp_bson_iter_next
etc, etc.
{noformat}
{quote}

summary: mongoc_uri_parse_database() may overlap with URI host component
description:
In PHPLIB-174, a user failed to escape the Unix domain socket path in their connection URI and received an odd exception message when database authentication failed. While reading the server logs, I realized that libmongoc was still connecting via a Unix domain socket, despite the socket path inadvertently getting picked up as {{uri->database}} in {{mongoc-uri.c}}.

I added some string dumping to {{mongoc_uri_parse_database()}} and {{mongoc_uri_append_host()}} and determined the following:

{noformat}
URI: mongodb://foo:bar@/tmp/mongodb-27017.sock/admin
HOST: /tmp/mongodb-27017.sock
DATABASE: tmp/mongodb-27017.sock:27017/
{noformat}

Although Unix domain sockets do need to be escaped per the [Connection String Specification|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/connection-string/connection-string-spec.rst], I found it odd that libmongoc was able to parse the host properly, but failed to parse the database. Since the order of URI components is clearly defined (e.g. credentials, host list, database, query string), database parsing should start _after_ the host list to ensure that component parsing never overlaps.

summary: Link error due to undefined reference to `assert'
description:
I'm not sure what changed in my system, because about two months ago, I built the C driver from source without problems. 

But after a pull and/or a fresh clone of the master branch, and several versions checked out, using cmake build, I get the following error:
{noformat}
libmongoc-1.0.so.1.7.0-dev: undefined reference to `assert'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
{noformat}

There were some files with the following warning:
{noformat}warning: implicit declaration of function ‘assert’ [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]{noformat}


Solution, if this is really an issue, is to include {{<assert.h>}} in the affected files. [This commit|https://github.com/beta-robots/mongo-c-driver/commit/8e1b6eca70010ed8c9ed5fec2fe4ef3639b11520] in facts shows the diff that works for me.

Btw, I'm building libbson from source as well.

Best

summary: mongoc_uri_unescape() silently fails on invalid percent-encoding
description:
[mongodb/specifications#105|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/105] was opened to address an ambiguity with the connection string spec and require "%" characters in the username or password component of a URI to be escaped.

While examining how libmongoc handles unescaping, I realized that {{mongoc_uri_unescape()}} appears to [fail silently|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.5.1/src/mongoc/mongoc-uri.c#L1349] if it encounters an invalid escape sequence. Should libmongoc at least log a warning before it returns {{NULL}} in this case?

summary: Detect socket API param types with Autotools for HP-UX
description:
mongoc_socklen_t must choice type on HPUX by checking _XOPEN_SOURCE_EXTENDED
More info here: https://curl.haxx.se/mail/lib-2009-04/0287.html

summary: release-compile task on Evergreen Windows variants builds debug version of driver
description:
From looking at the build logs of a [recent "release-compile" task on the "Windows (VS 2015)" builder|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_c_driver_windows_2015_release_compile_7c7e5bf733c3891628b0526d6a20fc2ec3a014d4_17_01_27_19_07_21], it looks like a debug build of the driver is being built by mistake.

The logs show the compiler flags "/MDd" and "/D _DEBUG" being passed to cl.exe, and the value of $\{CMAKE_CFG_INTDIR} appears to be "Debug".

Perhaps the "/p:Configuration=Release" option needs to be passed to msbuild.exe?

summary: Allow more detailed error reporting for failed URI parsing
Connection string documentation incomplete
description:
I was trying to find out what connection string options were supported and had a hard time doing so. Some observations:

* [Advanced Connections|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/advanced-connections.html] directs people to the MongoDB manual for information on additional connection options instead of to the mongoc_uri_t page, where most  of them are actually documented.
* On that last page, the "More information links at the bottom are duplicated and direct back to the index -- it's sort of circular.
* [mongoc_uri_t|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/mongoc_uri_t.html] docs don't mention authMechanism, but do have a function for it.   It appears that none of the [auth options|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/#authentication-options] in the MongoDB manual are documented in mongoc_uri_t docs.

Separately, there are "helper" functions for many URI options, but not all.  That's potentially confusing and perhaps worth documenting the rationale for why only some options have associated functions.

summary: Don't call getpid if we plan to overwrite result with taskid during ObjectId generation
description: _bson_context_init always calls getpid to generate an OID, then if possible and configured to do so, overwrites the result with the Linux thread "task id".

summary: syscall deprecated in macOS
test_create_index_fail error on zSeries Ubuntu without OpenSSL
Undeprecate bson_as_json
description: Consider *not* marking bson_as_json "deprecated", although it has been superseded by bson_as_extended_json. There are still legitimate uses for bson_as_json.
